summary: Created a brownfield architecture artifact for admin data ingestion at `_bmad-output/planning-artifacts/data-ingestion-admin-architecture.md`. The architecture deliberately constrains the MVP to batch indicator ingestion only, reuses the existing admin/auth/service/repository patterns, defines a backend `dry_run/apply` contract, keeps `replace_for_enrollment` semantics for existing indicator data, requires snapshot-before-apply, and introduces a dedicated execution audit store. Approval is still required on the main scope choices before implementation widens beyond this boundary.

decisions.title: MVP scope boundary
decisions.decision: Limit the MVP to indicator ingestion for existing student enrollments, not generic writes across all JSON stores.
decisions.rationale: This matches the current brownfield seed pattern and avoids broad architectural movement during stabilization.

decisions.title: Approved JSON targets
decisions.decision: Allow writes only to `backend/data/measurements.json`, `backend/data/checkpoints.json`, and a new `backend/data/admin_ingestion_executions.json` audit store.
decisions.rationale: The project context explicitly requires approved targets to be listed and forbids generic repository-wide writes.

decisions.title: Official source model
decisions.decision: Use an admin-uploaded structured JSON file as the official MVP source.
decisions.rationale: It satisfies the brief preference for a deterministic, validatable file and avoids exposing server paths in the UI.

decisions.title: API model
decisions.decision: Introduce a dedicated admin ingestion endpoint family around `POST /admin/ingestoes/indicadores`, with `mode=dry_run|apply` and optional rollback endpoint reservation.
decisions.rationale: This adds the new operation without breaking the existing `POST /admin/alunos/{student_id}/indicadores/carga-inicial` contract.

decisions.title: Persistence semantics
decisions.decision: Preserve existing `replace_for_enrollment(...)` behavior for persisted measurements and checkpoints.
decisions.rationale: Current repo behavior already replaces the enrollment slice; the architecture avoids introducing merge/history semantics in the same initiative.

decisions.title: Admin UI placement
decisions.decision: Add `Ingestao de Dados` as a new panel inside the existing `AdminPage`, using `/app/admin?panel=ingestao-dados` rather than a new top-level route.
decisions.rationale: This is the smallest safe frontend extension and preserves existing admin shell and routing conventions.

decisions.title: Operational safety
decisions.decision: Require server-side validation for both dry-run and apply, snapshot-before-apply via existing storage maintenance, storage I/O locking, and audit execution recording.
decisions.rationale: These are the core safety requirements from the brief and current project context.

decisions.title: Rollback posture
decisions.decision: Keep rollback operator-assisted first, backed by recorded snapshot references and audit updates, rather than making it a primary UI action in the MVP.
decisions.rationale: This avoids exposing sensitive path details and keeps rollback within the existing backend operational boundary.

risks.risk: The brief is broader than the MVP architecture boundary.
risks.mitigation: The artifact explicitly narrows scope to indicator ingestion and marks broader generic JSON ingestion as deferred.

risks.risk: Uploaded-file ingestion introduces a new multipart contract and audit store not present today.
risks.mitigation: The architecture keeps the change localized to existing admin, schema, service, and storage layers.

risks.risk: Preserving `replace_for_enrollment` may not satisfy future versioning/history expectations.
risks.mitigation: The artifact calls this out as an explicit approval point before implementation.

risks.risk: Current snapshot tooling backs up all registered stores, not only affected stores.
risks.mitigation: The architecture accepts global snapshots for MVP to reuse existing tooling and avoid parallel backup logic.

risks.risk: Rollback requirements could expand if a user-facing rollback action is later required.
risks.mitigation: The architecture reserves a rollback endpoint path but keeps initial delivery operator-assisted.
